adds.rationale: AI-compute pure-play; GTC 2022 keynote (March 22) unveiled the Hopper H100 data-center GPU architecture, a concrete commercial milestone aligned with the profile's 'ride the current AI wave' mandate, and the current watchlist's AI exposure is only via diversified mega-caps.

news_evidence.summary: NVIDIA announced GTC 2022 (March 21-24) with CEO Jensen Huang keynote and 900+ sessions on AI/HPC; major industry partners including Microsoft, Mercedes-Benz, AWS, Google Brain participating.
GTC 2022 keynote delivered March 22 covering new AI data-center products and accelerated-computing platforms.

adds.rationale: Direct uranium-fuel-cycle exposure complementing URA's miner-index; Cameco added 40M lbs to long-term contracts in early 2022 and the Russia-Ukraine war is forcing utilities globally to rebid origin-risk-free supply, a structural shift consistent with the profile's nuclear-near-term thesis.

news_evidence.summary: Cameco disclosed adding ~40 million pounds to its long-term uranium contract portfolio, signaling utilities locking in non-Russian supply.

Cameco working with Kazatomprom on alternate Inkai JV shipping routes that bypass Russian rail/ports; on-market RFPs from utilities re-emerging to reduce origin risk after Russia's invasion of Ukraine.

adds.rationale: Highest-quality robotics pure-play (robotic surgery, ~60%+ market share, high recurring-revenue mix) to complement BOTZ's diversified industrial/AI index exposure; da Vinci procedure growth continues to compound and the engineered-biology/medical-robotics intersection fits the profile's 'next waves' framing.

news_evidence.summary: Intuitive Surgical reported Q4 2021 results in late January 2022 showing strong da Vinci procedure growth and continued installed-base expansion, with management guiding to mid-teens procedure growth in 2022.
Industry coverage through Q1 2022 highlighted Intuitive's >60% share of soft-tissue robotic surgery and ~85% recurring revenue from instruments/services, framing it as a durable robotics franchise.

rationale_overall: Watchlist remains thin at 8 of 12, with the user's named 'next waves' (rockets, robotics, nuclear, quantum, engineered biology) covered only by single thematic ETFs. This quarterly call adds three pure-plays that deepen the AI, nuclear-fuel-cycle, and medical-robotics legs with concrete Q1 2022 evidence (NVDA's GTC/H100, Cameco's contract book and post-invasion utility RFPs, ISRG's procedure growth). Quantum and engineered-biology adds are deferred: IonQ/Rigetti are too thinly-traded post-SPAC and Rigetti just delayed its roadmap in March 2022, while CRISPR/Editas had no Q1 commercial inflection. No removes - the starter set is small and each ticker still earns its slot.
